response.justification: Bogdan has genuine geospatial/GIS domain exposure through GISCollective and Green Map work, and demonstrates distributed-systems intuition from high-throughput pipeline work at Dunnhumby. However, the role demands modern C++ (or Rust) as the primary language, and his C/C++ exposure is limited to a brief stint at National Instruments in 2014–2015 plus a thesis project — neither at the recency nor depth required for production routing pipeline engineering. His current commercial stack is entirely TypeScript/Node.js. There is also no evidence of routing tile generation, navigation guidance systems, or the kind of real-time map data transformation that is core to this position.
strengths: Hands-on geospatial/GIS platform development via GISCollective and Green Map, including a FOSS4G conference presentation on the tooling
Demonstrated distributed-systems thinking: built low-latency microservices handling millions of HTTP requests per second at Dunnhumby using async I/O and high-performance caching
Strong quality-assurance discipline evidenced by open-source D-language testing libraries and repeated TDD/craftsmanship advocacy across multiple roles
concerns: Primary commercial language is TypeScript/Node.js; C/C++ experience is a decade old and scoped to a single short engagement — no Rust experience mentioned anywhere
No routing tile generation, navigation guidance pipeline, or real-time traffic integration experience — the GIS work is data-collection and visualization, not routing optimization
Skills list omits C++ entirely, and the only geospatial pipeline output (GISCollective) is Node.js/Ember-based, signaling no practical path to the required C++ production codebase
